direction_seeds.seed: Adaptive N — learn to predict optimal sleep duration from context
Persistent S — carry fast weights across sequences instead of zero-initializing
Multi-scale sleep — hierarchical consolidation at token and chunk levels
Sleep vs depth — compare N-loop shallow vs non-looped deep under equal compute

purpose: Identify concrete follow-up research directions from the LLM Sleep paper
ranking_notes: Priority based on feasibility × impact. Low risk = easy to try.
search_limitations: Only directions directly inspired by the paper; no external successor search.

directions.title: Adaptive Sleep Duration (Learned N)
directions.trigger: Paper uses fixed N per run, but different contexts need different consolidation budgets
directions.why_promising: Adaptive N could reduce training overhead (short sleep for easy contexts) while maximizing benefit for hard contexts
directions.first_step: Train a lightweight auxiliary head that predicts optimal N from context complexity (entropy, novelty, cross-window dependency)

directions.title: Persistent Fast Weights Across Sequences
directions.trigger: SSM fast weights are zero-initialized at sequence start
directions.why_promising: Cross-sequence memory could enable lifelong learning and task adaptation without finetuning
directions.first_step: Initialize S from a learned prior (e.g., PCA of typical fast weights) instead of zero; test on continual reasoning tasks

directions.title: Multi-Scale Sleep (Hierarchical Consolidation)
directions.why_promising: Real data has hierarchical structure (tokens → phrases → episodes); multi-scale consolidation could capture this more naturally
directions.first_step: Design 2-level sleep: fast N within-window + slow M across-window consolidation with different SSM state update frequencies

directions.title: Attention-Informed Weight Consolidation
directions.trigger: Only SSM weights are updated during sleep
directions.why_promising: Attention patterns reveal which token relationships matter; this signal could guide weight updates in SSM blocks
directions.first_step: Use attention weights as gating coefficients in SSM update (e.g., S += β·attention_weight · v·k^T)

directions.title: Theoretical Characterization of Sleep Benefit
directions.trigger: Paper has no theoretical results linking N to reasoning capability
directions.why_promising: Formal bounds would guide architecture design and N selection without expensive search
directions.first_step: Prove that N-loop SSMs can simulate N additional steps of a Turing machine, while 1-loop cannot exceed a fixed depth cutoff (circuit complexity approach)

directions.title: Sleep vs Depth Head-to-Head
directions.trigger: Missing baseline: deeper non-looped model vs N-loop shallow model
directions.why_promising: Would disentangle whether the benefit is from recurrence or simply from more total compute at consolidation time
directions.first_step: Compare N=4 with D layers vs N=1 with 4D layers under same training token budget

directions.title: Inference-Time Sleep Adaptation
directions.why_promising: Test-time adaptation to new context distributions without gradient-based finetuning
directions.first_step: During inference, on detected distribution shift, run N sleep passes on recent context to adapt S before resuming prediction
